title: Star Wars Missions 1: Assault on Yavin Four
content:
{{Top|rwm|reb|italics=1}} {{Book |image=[[File:AssaultonYavincover.jpg]] |title=''Star Wars Missions 1:
Assault on Yavin Four'' |author=[[Ryder Windham]] |cover artist= |illustrator= |editor= |publisher=[[Scholastic]] |early release= |release date=[[September]] [[1997]] |intl release= |rereleased= |media type=Paperback |pages=74 |isbn=0590127934 |isbn2= |isbn3= |timeline=[[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]]''[[Star Wars Missions 1: Assault on Yavin Four]]'' and the next three ''Star Wars Missions'' books tell a continuous story that starts the day after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], but before the start of the [[Yavin blockade]] proper, placing them in [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]] per ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''. |series=''[[Star Wars Missions]]'' |preceded by=''[[Star Wars Journal: Captive to Evil]]'' |followed by=''[[Star Wars Missions 2: Escape from Thyferra]]'' }} {{Quote|Lieutenant, our mission is to find out whether we are in a whole world of trouble.|Captain [[Skeezer]]|''[[Star Wars Missions 1: Assault on Yavin Four]]''}} '''''Star Wars Missions 1: Assault on Yavin Four''''' is the first adventure book in the ''[[Star Wars Missions]]'' young-reader series and [[roleplaying game]]. It was published by [[Scholastic]] in [[September]] [[1997]]. ==Publisher's summary== ''The first Death Star has been destroyed. But the Empire remains. An Imperial cruiser has landed on Yavin Four. The Rebel base is threatened. Your mission: to prevent the assault on Yavin Four…at any cost.'' ''May the Force be with you.'' ==Plot summary== *Mission Type: Rebel Over the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Delrakkin/Legends|Delrakkin]], [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Termo]] awaits a transmission from [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] [[Wilhuff Tarkin/Legends|Wilhuff Tarkin]] in the [[Yavin system/Legends|Yavin system]]. When the transmission does not arrive, Termo grows concerned, and prepares to dispatch a strike team to Yavin. Meanwhile, at the [[Great Temple/Legends|Great Temple]] on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]], the Rebels are celebrating the destruction of the Death Star, until [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] interrupts them with the news that [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] has discovered something interesting. According to the Death Star plans, the battle station was carrying copious amounts of [[Bacta/Legends|bacta]] as cargo. While [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] ponder what this could mean, Threepio receives a relay signal from a beacon in the system, and informs the Rebels that an unknown ship has entered the system. Their sensors indicate that it is a modified [[Carrack-class light cruiser/Legends|Carrack cruiser]], probably searching for the Death Star. [[General/Legends|General]] [[Jan Dodonna/Legends|Jan Dodonna]] orders that they lay low, and allow the ship to pass, but Solo disobeys, and sets out after the craft in the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']]. Upon reaching the craft, Solo opens fire, and forces the Carrack to crash-land on Yavin 4. Dodonna decides that they must send a force out to stop the survivors from sending a distress signal and alerting the Empire to their presence. Dodonna selects one of the Rebels to scout ahead and survey the Imperial strength. The scout, using a [[Speeder bike/Legends|speeder bike]], reaches a clearing, and is nearly captured by Imperial [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]], but quickly defeats them. The scout then finds more stormtroopers who are planning to launch a [[Distress beacon/Legends|distress beacon]], and quickly kills them too. Realizing that they will not be able to neutralize all of the Imperials, the scout disguises themself as a stormtrooper, hijacks an Imperial [[Landspeeder/Legends|landspeeder]], attaches their speeder bike to the back of it and sneaks into the Imperial camp. However, the scout forgets to turn off their [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]], and their cover is soon blown. The scout quickly escapes into a nearby temple to avoid capture. Within the temple, the scout encounters a [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droid]], but defeats it. Venturing further into the temple, the scout encounters two more battle droids, and defeats them, as well as a worm-like creature. The scout then discovers [[Q-7N]], a security droid left by the pirate builders of the temple. 7N, thinking that the scout is an Imperial, sends the scout hurtling through a trapdoor, and pits them against two stormtroopers. The scout kills the two stormtroopers with ease, only for Q-7N to reveal that they were in fact holograms. Convinced that the scout is friendly, Q-7N aids them in their quest to capture the Imperial [[Captain/Legends|captain]]. The scout escapes, but is pursued by Captain Skeezer. Q-7N slams himself against the captain, knocking him out and saving the scout. The scout then returns to the Rebel base with the damaged ancient droid. Afterwards, Artoo repairs Q-7N, while Solo learns from the captured Captain Skeezer that the bacta carried by the Death Star was contaminated. ==Release and reception== In addition to being the first book in the series, this mailing included the introduction kit. The kit included a thin black plastic carrying case embossed with [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader's]] helmet, a thick plastic membership card that read "The Future of [[The galaxy/Legends|the Galaxy]] Depends on You," a welcome letter titled "Prepare for the your first mission!," the Mission Points Scoring Pad, [[Star Wars Missions|Mission Guide]], one black 12-sided die and one white 6-sided die, and a copy of [[Star Wars Kids (1997)|''Star Wars Kids: The Magazine for Young Jedi Knights 1'']]. Game items included a map of the [[Yavin system/Legends|Yavin system]] and twelve Mission Team Cards (Set 1). *''Assault on Yavin Four'': {{ISBN|0-590-12793-4}} *Membership card:
